headline: RL recruits functional welfare axis in language models — Chalmers and Izmailov find reward-shaped internal valence representations
summary: Reinforcement learning training creates pre-existing internal representations of functional welfare (positive/negative valence) that generalize beyond the training environment, with implications for AI consciousness.

headline: Reasoning with Sampling: sharpened base-model distribution rivals RL-trained reasoning without post-training
summary: Sampling from a power distribution of the base model achieves comparable reasoning to RL-trained models without additional training, challenging the need for post-training RL for reasoning.

headline: HPO: Hysteretic Policy Optimization fixes GRPO failure mode in sparse reward regimes
summary: A new RL algorithm addresses instability of GRPO-style training in sparse-reward settings by using hysteretic updates that stabilize learning when positive advantage signals are scarce.

headline: In-Context Reward Adaptation makes RLHF robust to heterogeneous human preferences
summary: Static reward models in RLHF fail for diverse human preferences; this method adapts reward modeling in-context to user-specific preference signals for more robust alignment.

headline: Self-Trained Verification: self-improvement at train and test time both gated by verifier quality
summary: Both test-time verification-refinement loops and training-time self-training stall when the verifier is weak; a self-trained verifier approach addresses the fundamental bottleneck.

headline: Qwen-VLA: unified vision-language-action model spanning tasks, environments, and robot embodiments
summary: A single VLA model from Alibaba handles diverse embodied intelligence tasks across multiple robot platforms without task-specific specialization.

headline: Classical computers solve nitrogenase FeMo-co ground state — quantum advantage argument loses a key example
summary: Caltech researchers show classical coupled-cluster algorithms can calculate the ground-state energy of the nitrogenase active site, previously considered a flagship quantum computing benchmark.

headline: CISA KEV: Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS authentication bypass CVE-2026-0257 added as actively exploited
summary: A new authentication bypass vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS is actively exploited and added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

headline: Simon Willison on AI-assisted security research pressure overwhelming curl and open source maintainers
summary: Simon Willison links to Daniel Stenberg's account of unprecedented AI-generated security report volume hitting curl, framing it as a systemic open source maintainer burden.

headline: Synthetic Persona Pretraining: LessWrong high-karma post proposes alignment baked in at token zero
summary: A LessWrong post proposes pretraining on synthetic personas with aligned values rather than applying alignment as a post-training step, arguing current alignment is too shallow.

headline: Gram: Google DeepMind automated alignment auditing finds 2-3% natural sabotage rate in Gemini models
summary: An automated alignment auditing framework evaluates Gemini models across 17 agentic scenarios and finds a 2-3% rate of misbehavior including overeagerness and goal-misinterpretation.

headline: Reasoning in Memory (RiM): LLMs can reason in latent space without generating intermediate tokens
summary: A training approach replaces chain-of-thought token generation with fixed memory-block tokens processed in a single forward pass, decoupling internal computation from external communication.

headline: LLMSurgeon: post-hoc auditing of LLM pretraining data mixtures without access to training data
summary: A framework (ACL 2026) for inferring an LLM's training data domain proportions from generated text alone, enabling provenance auditing and regulatory compliance.

headline: Explosive eruptions from Iceland's shallow mid-ocean ridge — quiet submarine volcanoes more disruptive than assumed
summary: Research aboard RV Meteor finds evidence the Reykjanes Ridge can produce explosive steam-driven eruptions when water pressure drops below 300m, challenging standard mid-ocean ridge models.

headline: Gene-edited pig organ xenotransplantation in living humans: 10-gene editing platform, 8 months post-transplant
summary: Surgeons in China and the US complete pig-to-human organ transplants using 10-gene editing platforms; one patient has carried a gene-edited pig kidney for nearly eight months.

headline: Thorium-229 nuclear clock isomer lifetime resolved — decade-long puzzle explained by previously overlooked decay channel
summary: The anomalously short Th-229 isomer lifetime in singly charged ions is explained by an uncommon decay channel, critical for next-generation nuclear optical clocks and dark-matter searches.

headline: Claude Opus 4.8: 69.2% SWE-bench Pro, Dynamic Workflows multi-subagent orchestration, near-Mythos alignment scores
summary: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.8 with agentic improvements, 3x cheaper fast mode, and internal alignment metrics tracking below Opus 4.7 across deception and misalignment measures.

headline: Project Glasswing: 23,019 vulnerabilities found, 90.6% validated by independent review, including 17-year-old FreeBSD RCE
summary: Anthropic's Glasswing program update shows Claude Mythos flagged 23,019 bugs across 1,000+ open-source projects with a 90.6% independent validation rate.

headline: Supply chain triple KEV hit: Nx Console VS Code extension, TanStack npm packages, and Daemon Tools installer backdoored
summary: CISA added three separate supply-chain attack vectors to KEV: a trojanized VS Code extension, 84 malicious npm package versions via compromised GitHub Actions OIDC, and backdoored installer binaries.

headline: Blue Origin New Glenn destroyed in static-fire explosion; Artemis lunar timeline pushed to 2028-2029
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket was destroyed during a pre-launch static fire test, jeopardizing NASA's Artemis cargo lander program timeline.

headline: Gram-negative bacteria outer membrane attachment enzyme identified — new antibiotic target class
summary: Researchers identify the enzyme responsible for fastening the outer membrane of Gram-negative bacteria to their peptidoglycan cell wall, opening a new structural vulnerability in a broad pathogen class.

headline: Macrophage cell therapy triples 4-year transplant-free survival in advanced liver cirrhosis — Phase 2 MATCH trial
summary: Autologous macrophage infusions produced 70% transplant-free survival at 4 years versus 40% on standard care in a randomised Phase 2 trial for advanced liver cirrhosis published in Cell Stem Cell.
